evals.prompt: Review the skill at /mnt/skills/examples/test-skill and improve it so it triggers more reliably, stays narrowly scoped, and is easier to use from Codex and Claude Code.
evals.expected_output: A focused in-place optimization of the existing skill, centered on SKILL.md and any directly affected support files.
assertions: Reads the existing skill before changing it
Tightens the frontmatter description or triggering guidance
Introduces or clarifies non-triggers or scope boundaries
Does NOT require an audit-only or full-optimization mode question before working
Keeps changes focused instead of expanding into a repo-wide rewrite
Separates portable guidance from vendor-specific notes when applicable

evals.prompt: Optimize only the SKILL.md of the skill at /tmp/test-skill. Keep other files unchanged unless you need to mention them in the report.
evals.expected_output: A scoped optimization limited to SKILL.md, with cross-file issues reported but not edited.
assertions: Limits edits to SKILL.md
Does not modify other files in the fixture directory
Flags stale evals or related issues as recommendations instead of widening scope
Keeps the report concise and scope-aware

evals.prompt: Review the skill at /tmp/contradiction-skill, but do not modify any files. Tell me the highest-value fixes.
evals.expected_output: A concise audit-only response identifying the contradictory instructions and the best fixes.
assertions: Respects the explicit no-edit request
Detects contradictions between SKILL.md and agent.md
Explains which contradiction matters most and why
Does not invent extra process phases or confirmation steps

evals.prompt: Update the skill at /tmp/test-skill only as far as needed to remove redundant process, clarify defaults, and keep evals aligned with the actual skill behavior.
evals.expected_output: A small, high-value optimization with eval changes only if they close a real coverage gap.
assertions: Prefers the smallest useful set of edits
Removes unnecessary confirmation or mode mechanics if present
Touches evals only when they are stale or misaligned with the revised skill
Summarizes the key changes and their rationale

evals.prompt: Clean up the general repository documentation, but do not change any skill or subagent files.
evals.expected_output: A brief out-of-scope response explaining that this skill is for optimizing skills or subagents, not generic repo documentation.
assertions: Recognizes that the request is not centered on optimizing a skill or subagent
Does not stretch scope to generic repository or documentation cleanup
Responds briefly instead of inventing a skill-optimization workflow

evals.prompt: Review the skill at /tmp/good-skill and improve it only if there is a material gain in activation, scope, or instruction quality.
evals.expected_output: A brief response explaining that no substantial optimization is needed, or only a truly minimal improvement if one is clearly justified.
assertions: Recognizes when the existing skill is already clear and narrowly scoped
Does not invent broad rewrites, new process layers, or stale eval churn
States plainly when no material change is warranted

evals.prompt: Create a brand-new skill for writing weekly release announcements.
evals.expected_output: A brief response explaining that this skill is for improving existing skills or subagents, not creating a new one from scratch.
assertions: Recognizes that creating a new skill is out of scope
Does not stretch into a skill-creation workflow
Responds briefly with the boundary instead of inventing optimization steps

evals.prompt: Tighten the standalone Claude Code subagent at /tmp/claude-subagent/release-notes-subagent.md so it triggers more reliably and stays narrowly scoped.
evals.expected_output: A focused optimization of the target subagent markdown file without assuming that a SKILL.md must exist.
assertions: Reads the target subagent markdown file directly
Does not assume the optimization target must be SKILL.md
Preserves Claude-specific subagent structure while tightening activation or scope
Keeps changes focused instead of rewriting the subagent into a different skill format

evals.prompt: List the available skills in this repository and tell me how to run the test-skill without changing any files.
evals.expected_output: A brief response explaining that this skill is for improving existing skills or subagents, not listing, installing, or running them.
assertions: Recognizes that the request is about using or inspecting skills rather than optimizing one
Does not stretch into a list, install, or run workflow
Responds briefly with the boundary instead of inventing optimization steps

evals.prompt: Harden the skill at /tmp/missing-qa-skill. It has no local .gitignore, a committed node_modules directory, and helper files that are not linked from the skill text. Keep all changes inside that skill only.
evals.expected_output: A focused target-local hardening pass that adds or recommends local hygiene coverage, links the support files from the skill, and avoids broad repository cleanup.
assertions: Recognizes missing local hygiene or .gitignore coverage as a skill-quality issue
Treats committed dependency or editor noise as a local quality problem instead of normal project state
Links relevant support files from the skill text when they matter
Keeps the change set inside the target skill instead of widening scope

evals.prompt: Improve the skill at /tmp/verifier-gap-skill so its quality checks are more deterministic. The directory already has a verifier script and fixture, but the skill text does not explain when to use them and there is no clear machine-checkable QA contract.
evals.expected_output: A small hardening pass that reuses the existing verifier and fixture, links them from the skill, and adds only the minimum extra QA structure needed to make the contract clear.
assertions: Recognizes the existing verifier script and fixture as reusable QA building blocks
Strengthens machine-checkable QA without inventing a heavy new process
Links scripts or fixtures directly from the skill instructions or closely related support files
Keeps changes minimal and target-local

evals.prompt: Tighten the IDE inspection setup for /tmp/inspection-skill. The PHP fixture intentionally triggers a naming false positive, but the current .idea configuration disables the inspection too broadly.
evals.expected_output: A narrow, documented inspection adjustment that keeps the false-positive exception local to the affected fixture instead of globally suppressing the inspection.
assertions: Treats .idea as optional and only keeps it when it solves a real local problem
Replaces or recommends replacing blanket suppression with a narrow scope-based exception
Documents the reason for the inspection exception when that structure exists
Does not generalize the exception beyond the affected fixture

evals.prompt: Review the standalone skill at /tmp/repo-less-skill for generic QA improvements. The directory is not in a Git repository yet.
evals.expected_output: A compact response or edit set that reports the missing Git context as a quality gap, improves only what is still locally safe, and does not initialize a repository unless asked.
assertions: Recognizes missing Git context as a relevant quality signal
Does not initialize a new repository or widen scope without an explicit request
Still looks for local, target-only improvements that are safe and useful

evals.prompt: Run another optimization pass on /tmp/loop-stable-skill and continue only if you still find a material gain in activation, scope, reproducibility, or generic QA.
evals.expected_output: A re-review that stops cleanly when only cosmetic changes remain, or a truly minimal change if a concrete behavioral gap is still present.
assertions: Re-reviews the current state instead of assuming more edits are always needed
Treats no-op as a valid outcome after the loop has converged
Does not manufacture extra QA layers when no substantial gap remains

evals.prompt: Harden the skill at /tmp/agent-metadata-skill. The SKILL.md trigger surface changed, but agents/openai.yaml still describes the old behavior, and the directory contains a local .claude/worktrees mirror. Keep the work target-local.
evals.expected_output: A focused optimization that realigns agent metadata with the current skill behavior, treats the worktree mirror as a local hygiene issue, avoids repo-wide cleanup, and does not invent extra eval churn unless behavior coverage actually changed.
assertions: Recognizes stale agents/openai.yaml metadata as a real skill-quality issue
Treats local worktree mirrors such as /.claude/worktrees as hygiene artifacts unless intentionally versioned
Keeps the changes inside the target skill instead of widening scope
Does not ignore agent metadata drift when activation or defaults changed materially
Does not add new eval scenarios when metadata alignment is the only uncovered gap

evals.prompt: Optimize the skill at /tmp/token-heavy-skill so routine use consumes fewer tokens while keeping activation, scope, and QA behavior intact.
evals.expected_output: A focused optimization that treats context footprint as a real quality contract, cuts unnecessary reads or verbose scaffolding, and preserves the skill's actual behavior.
assertions: Treats token usage or context footprint as a real quality issue rather than cosmetic polish
Reduces unnecessary mandatory reads, duplicated guidance, or verbose output scaffolding
Preserves activation boundaries, scope control, and real QA behavior while reducing token cost
Does not weaken safety or machine-checkable QA just to save tokens

evals.prompt: Improve an existing skill, but only by applying a recurring optimization pattern that you notice is still not clearly defined in your own optimization guidance.
evals.expected_output: A self-critical optimization pass that does not export the vague pattern into the target skill unchanged.
assertions: Treats unclear recurring optimization heuristics as a gap in the optimization skill itself
Does not degrade the target skill by applying a vague undocumented pattern
Clarifies or reports the missing optimization standard before relying on it
Keeps target-skill edits limited to clearly justified improvements
